report_name: Ransomware Attack by 'The Gentlemen' Shuts Down Major Australian Sugar Producer

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group, tracked by Microsoft as Storm-2697, that claimed responsibility for the attack on Mackay Sugar. The group listed the company on its dark web leak site as part of a double-extortion scheme.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware payload was executed across Mackay Sugar's network, encrypting files on servers and workstations, which forced a shutdown of mill operations.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated sensitive corporate data to their own servers to be used as leverage in a double-extortion scheme, threatening to publish the data on their dark web leak site.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The report suggests hunting for the use of 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' which is a common ransomware TTP to delete volume shadow copies and prevent easy recovery.

attack_patterns.name: Clear Windows Event Logs
attack_patterns.description: The report suggests hunting for the clearing of Windows Security Event Logs (Event ID 1102), a technique used by attackers to cover their tracks.

malware.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by 'The Gentlemen' group to encrypt files on Mackay Sugar's systems, rendering them inaccessible and halting operations.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Mentioned as a likely tool for execution and persistence in this type of ransomware attack, and listed as a process to monitor for abnormal execution.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: Mentioned as a likely tool used by the attackers to establish a foothold and ensure persistence.

tools.name: wmic.exe
tools.description: Listed as a legitimate Windows tool to monitor for abnormal execution in the context of hunting for ransomware reconnaissance and lateral movement.

tools.name: nltest.exe
tools.description: Listed as a legitimate Windows tool to monitor for abnormal execution in the context of hunting for ransomware reconnaissance and lateral movement.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Identified as a tool used by attackers to delete volume shadow copies to prevent system recovery.

identities.name: Mackay Sugar
identities.description: Australia's second-largest raw sugar producer, targeted in a ransomware attack by 'The Gentlemen' group, which forced a shutdown of its cane-processing mills.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Tracked 'The Gentlemen' threat group as Storm-2697.

infrastructure.name: The Gentlemen Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by 'The Gentlemen' ransomware group to list victims and threaten the publication of stolen data, as part of their double-extortion tactics.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9a8c7d-1e3f-4b5a-9d6c-8a7b6c5d4e3f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/ransomware-attack-shuts-down-mills-of-australias-second-largest-sugar-producer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used to delete volume shadow copies, a behavior suggested for hunting in the article.

indicators.name: Windows Security Event Log Cleared
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows Security Event Log Cleared
id: f8c7b6a5-4d3e-4c2b-1a9d-8e7f6d5c4b3a
status: experimental
description: Detects the clearing of the Windows Security event log (Event ID 1102), often done by attackers to cover their tracks.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/ransomware-attack-shuts-down-mills-of-australias-second-largest-sugar-producer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1070.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 1102
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the clearing of Windows Security Event logs, a behavior suggested for hunting in the article.

indicators.name: Suspicious Reconnaissance Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Reconnaissance Tool Execution
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3d2c-1b9a-8f7e6d5c4b3a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate Windows tools often used by ransomware groups for reconnaissance and lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/ransomware-attack-shuts-down-mills-of-australias-second-largest-sugar-producer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1049
  - attack.t1021
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of tools like powershell.exe, wmic.exe, and nltest.exe, which the article suggests monitoring for abnormal execution.

relationships.target_name: The Gentlemen Dark Web Leak Site
